_README: Asset manifest for Time Warp II: Conquest cutscenes. Two asset classes: (a) embedded fair-use commentary GIFs from Tenor — store URLs only, never redistribute the file; (b) self-hosted royalty-free / public-domain images from Wikimedia Commons / Pexels / Pixabay — download to memes/ and credit in credits page. Sandbox blocked direct downloads; URLs are validated and ready for a fetch script.

_schema.slot: Logical role in the game (which cutscene/character)
_schema.source_url: Canonical source page URL (Tenor view URL or Wikimedia file page)
_schema.media_url: Direct media URL for download or
_schema.license: PD | CC0 | CC-BY-2.0 | CC-BY-4.0 | CC-BY-SA-4.0 | fair-use-commentary
_schema.filename: Local path under memes/ if self-hosted; null for embed-only

yzma_wrong_lever.notes: PRIMARY pick — clearest 'WRONG LEVERRRR' shout moment. Use for greedy/over-reaching fails.
Variant — Kronk + Yzma at the lever.
Yzma close-up reaction — works as a static-feeling fail beat.

terry_tate.notes: PRIMARY tackle. Reskin in caption as 'PIRATE ENFORCER' — GIF unmodified.
Tackle-and-chase variant — feels like a mutineer being run down.
Wide-angle office charge — good for variety.

bobby_boucher.notes: PRIMARY Bobby tackle — running flying-tackle, perfect for boarding-party fails.
'Tacklin' fuel' — works for over-the-top brawl beat.

rickroll.notes: PRIMARY Rickroll — clean centered Rick Astley dance loop. Use as the 3-sec sting on every game-over.
Classic 'Never Gonna Give You Up' frame.

tulio_miguel.notes: PRIMARY Tulio + Miguel together — 'be gone' scheming gesture, perfect 'you got conned' energy.
'It can't get any worse' — soaking wet, defeated. Great for shipwreck fails.
Crying about lost gold — perfect for 'you lost the treasure' fails.
Chief Tannabok belly-laughing — the 'you got conned and they're laughing at you' beat.

both_is_good.notes: PRIMARY — Chel saying 'Both is good.' Perfect for indecision fails.

columbus_mutiny.notes: PRIMARY — period engraving of Spanish galleon in combat (Jurien de la Gravière, 1890). 2800x1728 PD. Use as full-bleed; overlay 'Y'ARRR — MUTINY!' headline in Bangers. Tutorial vibe means this should be more comedic than dark.
Backup — Cornelis Verbeeck (early 17th c.) Dutch/Spanish naval encounter. Very wide aspect ratio (47.6 × 141.6 cm) — letterbox-friendly. Pair with cannon SFX.

cortereal_fog.notes: PRIMARY — Theodor Kittelsen 1892 charcoal drawing of a ship in storm. Mood is exactly North Atlantic dread. Credit: Norwegian National Museum of Art (CC BY 4.0). Add fog overlay in CSS (linear-gradient) and slow-fade-to-black for the 'sailed north, did not sail back' beat.
Backup — modern photo of a ship engulfed in fog. Less period-correct but striking. Use only if the Kittelsen feels too painterly.

dagama_resistance.notes: Period engraving (Rijksmuseum) of da Gama's audience with the Zamorin of Calicut. Frames the locals as a court (which they were — sophisticated trading state), NOT as 'savages.' Karma frame: he came to extract, they pushed back. PARENT AGENT: verify direct media URL in file page before deploy.
Backup — generic period naval battle. Use overlay text 'THE OCEAN HAD OTHER PLANS' to keep blame on da Gama's hubris.

drake_dysentery.notes: PRIMARY — Spongebob in/near toilet. Cartoon, classroom-safe. Caption: 'Started from the bottom… ended on the toilet.' Aligns with the dysentery joke without being crude.
Bathroom dash variant — Spongebob running. Cartoony, totally safe.
Stomach-churn cartoon — totally PG. Use as the lead-in beat before the toilet punch.

magellan_mactan.notes: PRIMARY — period 16th-century woodcut depicting Magellan's death at Mactan. STYLIZED, NOT live-action. Frames Lapulapu's warriors with strategic competence. Caption frame: 'The locals had read the room.' PD-old (German source 1909, original 16th c.).
Backup — 19th-c. painting of the same scene. Slightly more dramatic, still stylized. PD.
Optional setup-shot before the death beat — Stradanus c.1592 allegory of the voyage. Ironic in retrospect since he didn't make it home.

narvaez_swamp.notes: PRIMARY — Currier & Ives clipper-in-hurricane lithograph. 5276x4094, very dramatic, color. Frame is 'the storm took them' (true to history — Narváez's raft was lost in a hurricane). PD US (pre-1931).
Optional setup card — the actual expedition route. '300 men in. 4 came out.' headline lands hard over the map. PARENT: verify URL hash on Wikimedia.

GAP: I could not find a clean classroom-safe Florida-mangrove painting on Wikimedia. The obvious hits (Thomas Moran 'Slave Hunt, Dismal Swamp') depict slavery and are inappropriate. RECOMMENDATION: Pixabay search 'mangrove sunset' or 'Florida swamp dusk' — Pixabay was 403-blocked from this sandbox. Suggested fallback: pair the hurricane image with a Bobby Boucher tackle GIF from the universal cast for the 'Bobby Boucher tackled by the swamp' joke.

raleigh_executioner.notes: PRIMARY setup shot — Henry Wallis 'Sir Walter Raleigh in the Tower' painting. Moody, candle-lit, NOT graphic. Use as the slow-burn before the cartoon-thunk punch. PARENT: verify URL hash on Wikimedia.
Period 1860 engraving of the execution — line art, NOT graphic. Charles Knight Popular History of England. Use as a wide-shot setup for a cartoon thunk-cut to black. Classroom-safe per content rules.

PRIMARY punch frame — Rijksmuseum CC0 etching of an executioner with sword on chopping block. Stylized 19th-c. line art. Apply CSS filter (high contrast / silhouette) to push it into Looney-Tunes territory. Pair with comic 'thunk' SFX. PARENT: verify URL hash on Wikimedia file page before deploy.

storm_galleon_bg.notes: SHOWPIECE — Ivan Aivazovsky, 1887, 'Ship in the Stormy Sea' (Hermitage, ЭРЖ-2344). 1063x1471 oil-on-canvas. Aivazovsky is THE master of dramatic seascapes — this is exactly the 'galleon at night, lit by lightning' vibe the hub needs. Public domain (artist d. 1900). Apply rgba(0,0,0,0.35) overlay so the title type pops in gold. PARENT: verify URL hash on Wikimedia file page before deploy.
Backup — Andries van Eertvelt (Flemish, d. 1652) 'Ships in Stormy Seas.' More period-correct (16-17th c. ships). Smaller (44.5x60cm). PD.

Backup — Ludolf Bakhuizen 1682 'Storm op de Hollandse kust.' Three-master in coastal storm. 2764x2254. Very period-correct. The painting is PD; the museum photo is CC BY-SA 4.0 by user Szilas — credit him.

jolly_roger.notes: Historical 'Old Roger' pirate flag — death holding hourglass + dart, striking heart. 4700x3600 PNG. CC BY-SA 4.0 by RootOfAllLight. Great as a hub UI accent / loading-screen flag. Credit on credits page.

parchment_texture.notes: PRIMARY parchment texture for credits page / how-to-play scroll. 2978x2240 by Caleb Kimbrough (Flickr). CC BY 2.0 — credit on credits page.

treasure_chest.notes: GAP: Wikimedia 'Treasure_chests' category was 404. Recommendation: Pixabay search 'treasure chest gold' (was sandbox-blocked here) or use OpenGameArt.org for a stylized chest sprite. Optional bonus — not blocking.

gaps_and_followups.issue: Sandbox blocked all curl + PowerShell network downloads
gaps_and_followups.impact: No files were physically pulled into memes/ subdirectories. URLs are validated and ready to fetch.
gaps_and_followups.fix: Run a download script with these direct URLs from a shell that allows outbound network. Self-hosted assets (Wikimedia) only — Tenor URLs should be embedded as-is, not downloaded.

gaps_and_followups.impact: Could not source modern stock photo + video alternatives for fog/swamp/treasure.
gaps_and_followups.fix: Michael (or a less-restricted agent) should run Pixabay searches for: 'pirate ship storm', 'galleon night', 'mangrove sunset', 'treasure chest gold'. All Pixabay content is royalty-free with no attribution required.

gaps_and_followups.issue: Narváez swamp landscape — no good Wikimedia source
gaps_and_followups.impact: Currently only the hurricane lithograph fills that slot.
gaps_and_followups.fix: Use a Pixabay mangrove/Everglades photo, OR pair the hurricane image with a Bobby Boucher universal-cast tackle for the 'tackled by the swamp' joke.

gaps_and_followups.issue: Some Wikimedia direct media URLs need hash verification
gaps_and_followups.impact: URLs marked 'PARENT: verify URL hash' have the file path but I could not extract the 2-char hash subfolder (e.g. /1/15/) for all of them from WebFetch summaries.
gaps_and_followups.fix: Before deploy, hit each Wikimedia file page and copy the canonical 'Original file' link, OR use the thumb URL pattern: https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/Special:FilePath/{Filename} which auto-redirects to the current file location.

gaps_and_followups.impact: Some Tenor URLs may be duplicate frames of the same source clip.
gaps_and_followups.fix: When the parent agent renders previews, drop any visually-identical pair and keep the highest-resolution one.
